Here is the entire list of my experience
1. Worst work life balance. You will be working for 12 hours a day.
2. Vice presidents and Senior Vice presidents doesn’t know anything. There are just there to consume lakhs of rupees salary monthly.
3. Whenever any project is assigned to you by VP or SVP there will not be any guidance or support given to you for BRD preparation and UAT test scenarios because senior management is only good for nothing.
4. Requirement understanding between IT, Business and BSG is not done systematically. Impact analysis, alternate solutions, actual working of system is not done. They are just blindly following what business is suggesting like a hotel waiter who writes the order and provides the food.
5. They are hiring 3rd party payroll testers whom they give 15-20k salary without giving proper training and if testers salary is increased after few years then they will be asked to leave the bank and work for some other client.
6. Whenever year end budget is released then entire chunk is consumed by senior management and peanuts are given to junior staff who work hard.
7. I request everyone to work for some other company like technical support or customer support role. Atleast you will be happy and motivated over there.
8. Business analyst role is just for name sake. Actually we are doing support role and no growth in future due to high level politics.

review-details_detailsLink__NxIv5: Insight into Business Analyst Role at Axis Bank
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9:
Pros:

Challenging Projects: At Axis Bank, I was involved in diverse projects spanning across different areas of banking operations. This provided me with exposure to a wide range of challenges and opportunities to apply my analytical skills.

Cross-Functional Collaboration: One of the highlights of working at Axis Bank was the collaborative environment. I had the chance to work closely with teams across departments, including IT, finance, and marketing, which enhanced my understanding of the business ecosystem.

Learning and Development: Axis Bank prioritizes employee growth and development. Through regular training sessions, workshops, and access to industry resources, I was able to continuously enhance my skills and stay updated with the latest trends in the banking sector.

Responsibility and Autonomy: As a Business Analyst, I was entrusted with significant responsibilities and given the autonomy to drive initiatives forward. This empowered me to take ownership of projects and make meaningful contributions to the organization.

Career Growth Opportunities: Axis Bank offers ample opportunities for career advancement. The organization encourages internal mobility and provides support for employees to explore different roles and departments, fostering professional growth and development.
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2:
Work-Life Balance: Like many roles in the banking industry, maintaining a healthy work-life balance can be challenging at times, especially during peak periods or when deadlines are tight. However, with effective time management and prioritization, it is manageable.

Hierarchical Structure: The hierarchical structure within the organization may lead to bureaucratic processes and decision-making, which can sometimes slow down the pace of projects. However, this is a common aspect in large organizations and can be overcome with effective communication and collaboration.
Overall, my experience as a Business Analyst at Axis Bank was rewarding and contributed significantly to my professional growth. The exposure to diverse projects, collaborative work environment, and opportunities for learning and development made it a valuable experience. I would recommend Axis Bank as an employer for individuals seeking a challenging and dynamic career in the banking sector.

review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9: Axis Bank is one of the leading private sector banks in India, offering a wide range of banking and financial products and services. Over the years, the bank has established itself as a trusted name in the industry, thanks to its customer-centric approach, innovative products, and technology-driven solutions. One of the key strengths of Axis Bank is its robust technology platform, which enables customers to access a wide range of banking services through various channels, including mobile banking, internet banking, ATMs, and branches. The bank has also launched several innovative digital products, such as Axis Direct, a trading and investment platform, and Axis Pay, a mobile payment app. Axis Bank offers a wide range of banking and financial products, including savings accounts, current accounts, credit cards, loans, and insurance products. The bank has also established itself as a leading player in the retail lending segment, offering home loans, personal loans, and auto loans at competitive interest rates. In terms of customer service, Axis Bank has a well-trained and dedicated team of customer service executives who are always ready to assist customers with their queries and concerns. The bank has also set up a robust grievance redressal mechanism, which ensures that customer complaints are resolved promptly and efficiently. Overall, Axis Bank is a trusted and reliable banking partner for customers who are looking for a wide range of banking products and services backed by technology-driven solutions and excellent customer service.

Banking industry seems a glorified industry to work for if u don't know the ins and outs of it.
Most people confuse Pvt. Banks with Govt banks.

# Long working hours; its a 9 to 8-8:30 hour job
# Focus on third party products such as insurance more than daily workings of a bank.
# The bank will not entertain customers unless and untill they are potential Insurance buyer.
# the bank Does Not Value Employees, Slow Promotions and Fast Demotions.
# Its a Wall of Fame or a Walk of Shame kind of situation.
# No job security.
# Fake promises by the Organisation. I didn't get the pay that I was promised during recruitment.
# Office Politics. Senior management ignores or does not care about lower management.
# Abuse of power.
# No benefits.
# HR ignores senior management abusing their power. No respect is given to the Women. Employees have to handle constant disrespect and humiliation.
# No Incentive.

I have lost Faith and Trust in Private Banks since joining the one. No wonder the stock prices are crashing.

review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2:
Actually the cons are more related to the reporting authorities and I have less complaints with the bank as such. 
1) There is a tendency of all reporting managers to give ratings as per their own perceptions and even actual achievements as give in PMS are ignored by them & HR does nothing about that. Unfortunately all the performers are easily branded as 3 (on a rating scale of 5) along with the average performers. Top ratings of 4 or 5 are 'reserved' for employees with higher grades & favorites of bosses. 
2) Organization is becoming increasingly bureaucratic. You can only get your work done if you are at a higher grade. Grades not job profile are an important factor while working in here. Even NBFCs are more professional in this regard!!
3) There are two groups of employees. The first group comprises of senior guys who joined 8-10 years back mostly from nationalized banks. Second group are all the others. First group commands all the authority and make working tough for all the others.
4) Employees working in Mumbai - CO are working in a different wonderland and are aloof from all the ground realities. Thanks to MD, HR has launched a fancy 'Axis Ahead' program to hire MTs from Premier B-Schools at high packages. These MTs are placed in CO & become AVP within 2 years of joining but have zero knowledge of banking!!!

review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9: They want employees to learn new technologies
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2:
1.Typical BOSS IS ALWAYS RIGHT culture,encourages nepotism .I have seen people taking up projects even outside their portfolio,just to impress one person who basically controls the whole office ,in return they get favours in promotions and appraisals .To impress him ,they even help him with non-offcial work. Obviously the additional workload is passed on to subordinates like us who end up working on weekends  while they walk away with the credit and appraisals.

2.Your boss can say anything to you  ,nobody can question him.
If you try to raise your concerns about work ,his behaviour or anything in general ,they will shout and embarass you in front of the whole team, and threaten to give a low rating .I personally felt like crying sometimes. They  can go to any extent to get the work done. KEEP YOUR SUPERIORS IMPRESSED AND YOUR SUBORDINATES SCARED,THEY ARE YOUR SLAVES 

3.I could never understand the difference in salary between a DM and a M grade Business Analyst,when the roles and responsibilities are essentially the same .Slight difference is acceptable ,but 50% + difference in salary for the same work!!!! What logic is this ?
Again,all M grade BAs got a salary revision last year ( over and above the performance appraisal ) ,why not DMs as well?
Is this a peasant - landlord system where M is the landlord and DM is the peasant 

4.Salary has a useless loan component ,which basically reduces your actual salary to way below the industry standard.

5.Another thing I will never be able to understand is the obsession with IIT.
FRESHERS FROM IIT ARE OFFERED A M GRADE ,WHILE LATERALS WITH EXPERIENCE ARE OFFERED A DM GRADE!!!! Again ,any logic ?????

6.Appraisals - A BLACK BOX!!!Your work does not matter ,dont know what formula  on  earth they apply to calculate the ratings,but again,if you are a fresher or an IITIAN, you will get promoted and get a good appraisal .
Laterals who joined as DMs are basically screwed here as well.
50% of the employees have a surprised face at the end of the process - both pleasant and unpleasant surprises .
And which company in the world ,gives a ZERO APPRAISAL? ,even inflation rate is more than that !!!!
This is derogatory and demoralising for the employee who has worked hard for an entire year .

7. The approvals and permissions required for getting any work done remind me of a sarkari Babu office .Frankly ,the processes in place are outdated and are a roadblock for work .On  top of that IT team deliberately dont help and delay your work just to satisfy their ego. IT TAKES A FEW MONTHS JUST  TO GET A BANK ACCOUNT AND A LAPTOP TO WORK

8.Don't even get me started on the infrastructure !!

9.No HR in office ,if you are try reaching the Mumbai office HR,they don't pick up the phone.Drop a mail ,if you are lucky,I repeat, if you are lucky you will get a reply within 15 days,otherwise no reply

review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2:
Where do I start?

If you're in IT (Information Technology) as a Software Engineer or software developer, DO NOT JOIN THIS BANK! RUN AWAY! FAR AWAY! There are absoltely NO opportunities for IT people in here. 

The management sucks in this bank. No really, they do! They  LIE to your face! They keep making up stories about having so much work that they can't do any of it without help.  In reality, there is NO WORK and ALL software people I have seen spend more time on their smartphones than on their actual work. Why? Because THERE IS NO WORK! Your skills will rot, you will become used to being lazy and all you will hear is management lying to your face.

If you take a leave and your manager doesn't approve it in time, God Help You! May Buddha Bless You because this idio-tic and stu-pid and worth-less bank WILL mark the day you took leave as a LOP day. Your salary will get cut for that! Say goodbye to your financial plans for buying a motorcycle! You will be paid back that amount but ONLY in the NEXT month end! Until then you cannot do anything. Now you might start wondering what the hell am I doing here. Wleocme to the club!

The bank DOES NOT value you! You are NOTHING to them! You are just a number and nobody cares about you. I have seen departments literally fighting because of some issue in a software. People hate each other and nothing gets done without threatening ohers.

The bank is SO CHEAP and useless that they can't even spend money on Microsoft Office products. They use freeware like Open Office to write their documenbts. Smaller companies with LESS revenues have the latest Microsoft Office products! And don't get me started on the WhatsApp culture there. All OFFICIAL work is done THROUGH WHATSAPP! What? Whatsapp? yes! There are more WhatsApp groups than actual departments.

The turnover rates  are very high. 90% of the people in the IT department in the bank are contractors and they leave in droves. Even some poor sap who joins will leave after a bit. Employees themselves are quitting left right and center after the last appraisal. Nobody got hikes and even if they did, they got 1%. 

There was a very prominent firing recently and this pathetic company is still struggling to recover.....and I sough DON'T think it ever will recover! Good to know!

if you join, you may be asked to travel to ANY location the stupid bank has its branch in! Even if you are living in the Western Suburbs of Mmbai, you may be asked to travel regulary to the South of Mumbai like Worli or Dadar at YOUR OWN EXPENSE. You cannot book a cab and even if you do and try for reimbursement, you will have to butter up your supervisor a lot before they accept.

With all this, WHY do you want to join this bank in IT and ruin your peace of mind? Look elsehwere!

review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2:
Can't speak for other depts.  But IT dept I can bet is the worst. 

Let me first speak in general about the onboarding process.
In Mumbai there are 4 offices now in total, Worli, Andheri, Airoli and Dadar. The management have no idea beforehand where to put any new recruit.
The roles offered in Job Description should rather be slaves to AVPs, DVPs etc. than Software Developer, which btw was actually the role in JD for IT people (for tier 1 colleges) and in offer letter it was mentioned Business System Analyst. Anyway they may ask you any time to go to any of these offices. If questioned, they have the most dull, cliched reply - "You should not have travel issues in Mumbai". Also they'll project a fancy picture of what they do during induction which is hardly what they do.

Now the IT-dept. 

First of all this dept. has no need of freshers is what it seems like.  They have been doing everything the age-old way and they need experienced people mostly. The Reporting Managers had no idea about new joinees on the first day and it felt like they really didn't expect freshers. So clearly the requirement was not conveyed effectively to the management or they didn't bother to even discuss. Anyway what you're made to do is provide support for already existing applications and if at all there is any development involved, it is handled by vendors.
While you're mostly involved in slavery, like taking orders and getting work done. Get server access, port opening, raising requests, mailing people, calling teams, following up and a long list of clerical works is all you'll get to do.  There is no training provided whatsoever. Old Employees, AVPs, DVPs resort to dictatorship, adamant about incorporating any new ideas, use age-old methods and are highly unprofessional.
There is no development work, so if you want to learn something, please don't come here. You'll get to work on aftermath of everything and that too you'll have no prior knowledge about. Just get into and it and get it done.
